book_claims.claim: Used in multiple Longevity Diet dishes including spinach with raisins and Brussels sprouts.

supplementary_research.source: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry, 2008 (PMID 18570414)
supplementary_research.finding: Raisins significantly increase iron bioavailability from spinach; fructose and organic acids enhance non-heme iron absorption by 2-3× compared to spinach alone.
Spinach-raisin combinations are a traditional Mediterranean dish; the sweet-savory profile promotes higher vegetable consumption and micronutrient diversity.

culinary_pairings.tradition: Catalan espinacs amb panses i pinyons
Italian sautéed spinach with garlic and raisins

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Absorption increased 2-3× when combined with raisins; cooking reduces oxalate, further improving iron absorption
Fat-soluble carotenoids; absorption doubled when served with olive oil; protective against age-related macular degeneration
Fructose enhances iron chelation; resveratrol and quercetin add anti-inflammatory effects

synergies.description: Fat in olive oil dramatically increases lutein and zeaxanthin bioavailability from spinach
Allicin from garlic and iron from spinach support immune function; garlic enhances spinach's folate retention
Pine nuts provide fat for carotenoid absorption and add pinoleic acid, complementing spinach's micronutrient density
